body: It failed again, we still don't have enough e2e, integration, unit tests. Also there is no need to print the command that will be executed in the message before it, Formal AI just needs to explain what it is about to do, and command anyway printed by OpenCode itself when actually executed. `so I can verify the next step before continuing.` does not produce any meaningful information, we should write actually useful output to the user, so at any step he understands what is going on, and it is not robotic but actually useful and natural conversation. Here are examples by claude: and by codex: In our contributing, and testing guide lines we must write, that it is good idea to spawn separate claude/codex or opencode (with free models) and other available agents to have some examples on how they work with similar prompts/requests. So we can take best practices from them. We should not try to do too complex behaviour, yet we should make it natural and easy to understand for the user, and do mininum actions to fullfil the need of the user. Please deeply redo the analysis make sure all parts of the system involded in that simple prompt and Formal AI issue reporting are fully working and so on. Please test as much as you can yourself, and don't waste my time anymore on testing.
